text: Hashing has been widely used in multimedia similarity search applications because of the storage efficiency and fast search speed. In recent years, multimodal hashing for complex data like image-tag pairs has attracted increasing attention. A large number of practical applications provide supervised semantic labels of media samples. Therefore, improving the search accuracy is crucial, which motivates the development of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods. However, many existing SMH methods are inefficient for large-scale search applications, because their training time complexity is still high. In this paper, we propose a lightweight Supervised Multimodal Hashing (SMH) framework named Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), which not only explores the relationships between heterogeneous data samples and their corresponding semantic labels but also realizes a trade-off between the modeling capability for large-scale data and the training speed. The experimental results on the widely used large-scale image search benchmarks (Scene15 and MIR-Flickr-500) confirm the superiority of SCM in terms of search accuracy and speed compared with the state-of-the-art hashing baselines.
